content.problemStatement:
Chef's good friend Shubham has an assignment to complete, but he is too lazy to do it, so he asked Chef to help him. On the other hand, Chef was busy in the kitchen, so he in turn asked you to help Shubham.

You are given a sequence of positive integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã and an integer KKK. For any positive integer XXX such that exactly KKK bits in the binary representation of XXX are equal to 111, consider the sum S=‚àëi=1N(X‚àßAi)S = \sum_{i=1}^N (X \wedge A_i)S=‚àëi=1N‚Äã(X‚àßAi‚Äã); here, ‚àß\wedge‚àß denotes bitwise AND. You should choose XXX in such a way that SSS is maximum possible. If there is more than one such value of XXX, you should find the smallest one.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the smallest possible value of XXX.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Exactly one bit in XXX should be 111. Our options are:

If X=1X = 1X=1, S=1+0+1+1=3S = 1+0+1+1 = 3S=1+0+1+1=3.
If X=2X = 2X=2, S=2+0+0+0=2S = 2+0+0+0 = 2S=2+0+0+0=2.
If X=4X = 4X=4, S=0+4+4+0=8S = 0+4+4+0 = 8S=0+4+4+0=8.
For any greater valid XXX, S=0S = 0S=0.

The maximum value of SSS is 888, so the answer is the only value of XXX where we get S=8S = 8S=8.
